question: This 2-second audio sample is derived from the CWRU Bearing Dataset. Telemetry was recorded at 48 kHz under a 1 HP load and a shaft speed of 1772 rpm, using accelerometers installed at the Drive End (DE) and Fan End (FE) of the motor. Each audio segment combines 1 second from the DE sensor and 1 second from the FE sensor. Based on the following time-domain statistical features   max: 0.6634, min: -0.5789, mean: 0.0244, sd: 0.1165, rms: 0.1191, skewness: -0.0679, kurtosis: 0.6325, crest: 5.5716, form: 1.2803   which fault type does this sample most likely correspond to?
COT: <think> Low overall energy (sd = 0.1165, rms = 0.1191) and small amplitudes (max = 0.6634, min = -0.5789) combine with very low impulsiveness (kurtosis = 0.6325) and a modest crest factor (crest = 5.5716); near-symmetric shape (skewness = -0.0679) and stable waveform form (form = 1.2803) point to a mild rolling-element defect at the smallest ball size; based on the above analysis, the most likely fault type is Ball Fault 0.007 inches. </think>

Each audio segment combines 1 second from the DE sensor and 1 second from the FE sensor. Based on the following time-domain statistical features   max: 2.6306, min: -2.7466, mean: 0.0197, sd: 0.4320, rms: 0.4324, skewness: -0.0184, kurtosis: 3.0613, crest: 6.0831, form: 1.4458   which fault type does this sample most likely correspond to?
COT: <think> Very high broadband energy (sd = 0.4320, rms = 0.4324) with modest kurtosis (kurtosis = 3.0613) and crest (crest = 6.0831) and near-zero skewness (-0.0184) suggests a larger but smoother inner-race defect; based on the above analysis, the most likely fault type is Inner Fault 0.021 inches. </think>

Each audio segment combines 1 second from the DE sensor and 1 second from the FE sensor. Based on the following time-domain statistical features   max: 3.1870, min: -2.8553, mean: 0.0212, sd: 0.4428, rms: 0.4433, skewness: -0.0271, kurtosis: 3.1437, crest: 7.1885, form: 1.4430   which fault type does this sample most likely correspond to?
COT: <think> Very high broadband energy (sd = 0.4428, rms = 0.4433) with modest kurtosis (kurtosis = 3.1437) and crest (crest = 7.1885) and near-zero skewness (-0.0271) suggests a larger but smoother inner-race defect; based on the above analysis, the most likely fault type is Inner Fault 0.021 inches. </think>

Each audio segment combines 1 second from the DE sensor and 1 second from the FE sensor. Based on the following time-domain statistical features   max: 2.9012, min: -3.0009, mean: 0.0224, sd: 0.4670, rms: 0.4675, skewness: -0.0050, kurtosis: 3.3685, crest: 6.2057, form: 1.4732   which fault type does this sample most likely correspond to?
COT: <think> Very high broadband energy (sd = 0.4670, rms = 0.4675) with modest kurtosis (kurtosis = 3.3685) and crest (crest = 6.2057) and near-zero skewness (-0.0050) suggests a larger but smoother inner-race defect; based on the above analysis, the most likely fault type is Inner Fault 0.021 inches. </think>
